content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy explicitly states "we do not collect, process, or store your location data," directly contradicting RunSessionManager.swift's use of CLLocationManager (requestWhenInUseAuthorization/startUpdatingLocation) and the app description's claim of GPS-based run mapping (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt:1).
Pierre: Apple reviewers cross-check the privacy policy text against actual app behavior, and a direct contradiction like this ("we do not collect location" vs. active CoreLocation tracking used for the app's core GPS-mapping feature) is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger. You need to update the privacy policy at runtracker.example/privacy to accurately disclose location collection, its purpose (route/pace tracking), storage (device/iCloud), and retention/deletion options, and ensure this matches the App Privacy nutrition label in App Store Connect.
